title: grewia denticulata
intro: Grewia denticulata, also known as the Toothed Grewia or Indian plum, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Grewia denticulata include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Grewia denticulata include infusions, decoctions, and extracts that can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams, although further research is required to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
